events.title: TTF Bengaluru Expo Begins
events.description: TTF Bengaluru — Travel and Tourism Fair — is a 3-day expo bringing together travel brands, tour operators, hoteliers, and travel enthusiasts, celebrating the joy of exploration and the business of wanderlust.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: TTF Bengaluru is a 3-day travel expo — genZScore 5-6. The crowd is mixed — travel industry professionals, travel bloggers, travel enthusiasts, and GenZ wanderlust types. Two distinct fashion modes: (1) industry/professional attendees — smart casual, business casual, presentable but not formal; (2) travel enthusiast visitors — travel-inspired, practical yet stylish, wanderlust aesthetic. The dominant fashion language is travel-ready — lightweight, versatile, layerable, comfortable for a full day of walking an expo floor. Think travel blogger aesthetic — linen shirts, utility trousers, comfortable co-ords, versatile layers. Colors are inspired by travel destinations — earthy neutrals, ocean blues, terracotta, olive, warm beige. No heavy ethnic, no clubbing energy. The Bengaluru crowd adds a trend-forward spin — clean silhouettes, interesting textures, practical but considered.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: TTF Bengaluru is the travel blogger meets industry professional fashion moment — practical, put-together, and quietly stylish. Lead with linen shirts in olive, navy, or beige for men — paired with cargo or straight-fit chinos. Women in a clean linen co-ord, a relaxed printed dress, or a utility-inspired top with wide-leg trousers. Layers are key for an expo floor — a lightweight jacket or overshirt adds versatility across 3 days. Cargo trousers and utility-detail pieces are uniquely relevant here — travel context makes functional pockets a genuine style choice not just a practicality. The travel blogger aesthetic means interesting destination-inspired prints and earthy tones. Comfortable implied footwear means ankle-length and above-knee hemlines work best for all-day walking. Tote bags and crossbody bags are essential — expo floor means hands-full moments. Caps for the practical traveller. Minimal jewellery — a simple watch, small earrings, one bracelet. Clean and versatile across all 3 expo days.

app.headline: Travel Fair Fits, Bengaluru Edition.
lines: Linen, cargo, and a tote — expo floor uniform sorted.
Dress like the next destination is already booked.
Three days, one travel fair, zero basic fits.

social_media.headline: Dressed For The Journey, Not Just The Expo.
lines: POV: walked into TTF Bengaluru in an olive linen co-ord and looked like a travel documentary.
Travel expo fit check — earthy tones, cargo details, a bag that fits everything.

The concert energy is warm, feel-good, and celebratory rather than dark or edgy. This is a mid-tier dressy event — not as elevated as a fine dining date, not as casual as a college hangout. events.genres: Music, Live Concert, Indie, Singer-Songwriter, Folk
events.description: Osho Jain brings his signature indie singer-songwriter sound live — an intimate night of honest lyrics, gentle guitar, and the kind of music that feels like a conversation between the stage and your soul.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Osho Jain is a beloved indie singer-songwriter — genZScore 6-7. His audience is emotionally intelligent, aesthetically aware GenZ and millennials who appreciate honest storytelling in music and intentional dressing. Very similar universe to Anuv Jain but with a slightly more casual, less polished energy — more coffeehouse indie than concert hall. The fashion mood sits at warm casual meets indie-thoughtful. Earth tones, warm neutrals, interesting textures, vintage-inspired pieces. Think linen shirts, corduroy, knit tops, relaxed co-ords, flowy silhouettes. This is a standing or seated intimate venue — comfortable but considered. Colors are warm and muted — olive, rust, caramel, sage, warm beige, dusty rose, brown. No loud prints, no clubbing energy, no heavy ethnic. The crowd dresses like they discovered this artist before anyone else and their outfit proves it.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Osho Jain live is the coffeehouse indie aesthetic in its purest form — dress like the music, warm and honest. Women in a rust or sage flowy dress, a relaxed linen co-ord in earthy tones, or a knit top with wide-leg corduroy trousers. Men in a well-worn linen or cotton shirt in olive or caramel — half tucked, sleeves rolled — with straight-fit jeans or chinos. The texture story matters here — linen, corduroy, and knit fabrics feel right for this intimate setting. A light overshirt or jacket for the Bengaluru March evening. Dainty artisanal jewellery — thin gold chains, small hoop earrings, a simple ring stack. Canvas tote or small leather-look crossbody bag. Vintage-inspired or round glasses. Gingham and subtle check shirts are particularly on-brand for this crowd. The overall energy is — effortlessly warm, casually considered, dressed like the music is already playing in your head.

events.description: Dhurandhar returns — a high-octane Bollywood action thriller built on vengeance, power, and the kind of raw cinematic energy that makes every frame feel like a reckoning. The revenge has a name and it arrives March 18th.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Dhurandhar The Revenge is a Bollywood action thriller — genZScore 7-8. The title and genre signal a dark, intense, power-driven aesthetic — the kind of film where the protagonist is both feared and respected. The Bollywood action thriller crowd in India dresses with maximum intention for premieres — the fan base is passionate, style-conscious, and deeply invested in the hero's aesthetic. Character fashion references for a revenge thriller: (1) The protagonist Dhurandhar — the vengeful hero, dark and powerful aesthetic, structured blacks and deep navy, intensity in every silhouette; (2) The antagonist world — dark, opulent, and menacing — deep maroon, charcoal, and gold excess; (3) The revenge thriller tone — dark, brooding, and cinematic. March in Bengaluru is warming up toward summer — the transitional weather makes light layering still possible but heavy fabrics are being set aside. The Bollywood connection means the screening crowd dresses up significantly — smart casual to elevated, with a dark and powerful color palette. Dark navy, charcoal grey, deep maroon, black, and occasional gold accents are the most catalog-relevant colors. The action thriller genre opens tactical and structured fashion directions alongside the elevated Bollywood party aesthetic.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Dhurandhar The Revenge screening is a dark, powerful Bollywood fashion moment — dress like the protagonist just walked into the room and everyone went quiet. The hero aesthetic is the dominant catalog direction — structured, dark, and intentional. Men in a sharp black or midnight navy suit with a crisp white or deep red shirt — the Bollywood action hero screening uniform at its most powerful. A dark blazer with slim black trousers and a turtleneck in charcoal or deep maroon is equally cinematic. Pocket squares in gold or red for the elevated men's detail. Women in a deep burgundy or midnight blue satin co-ord, a structured black dress with gold accessories, or a dark dramatic silhouette that matches the film's intensity. The antagonist direction — deep maroon with gold excess — is the most editorial screening choice for women. Chain jewellery in gold or silver is the most revenge-thriller-coded accessory — layered chains, bold rings, and statement watches. The March Bengaluru transitional weather makes a structured blazer or dark jacket the perfect layer. Dark sunglasses for the premiere swagger. The overall energy is — dressed like you walked in knowing exactly what Dhurandhar came to do.

app.headline: The Revenge Has A Dress Code.

events.title: Navratri Begins (Day 1)
events.description: Navratri begins — nine nights of Goddess Durga's celebration exploding in garba circles, dandiya beats, chaniya cholis, and the most colorful ethnic fashion moment of the year. Day 1 sets the tone — the first twirl of nine.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Navratri is THE ethnic fashion event of the year for GenZ — genZScore 7-9. Nine nights of garba and dandiya mean nine distinct outfit moments — this is the festival where GenZ plans outfits weeks in advance. Day 1 sets the energy — vibrant, festive, dance-ready. The chaniya choli and lehenga choli are the undisputed hero categories. Garba requires full twirl-ready silhouettes — flared lehengas, chaniya cholis with multiple layers, mirror-work and embroidery that catches the light as you spin. The GenZ Navratri crowd has elevated this into a full fashion competition — coordinated duos, color-of-the-day dressing, statement blouse designs. Men in kediya-kurta with churidar or straight-fit ethnic pants. Colors follow the nine-day color code — Day 1 is typically royal blue or yellow depending on the regional tradition. Mirror-work, gota-patti, bandhej, and heavy embroidery are the surface styling signatures. This is the most jewellery-intense ethnic fashion moment of the calendar.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Navratri Day 1 — the first twirl sets the tone for nine nights. Chaniya choli is the absolute hero category — full flared skirts with mirror-work and embroidery that come alive when you spin. The more layers, the better the garba silhouette. Women in a heavily embellished chaniya choli in royal blue or yellow for Day 1 color code, or a lehenga choli with gota-patti detailing and a matching dupatta. The blouse design is a key fashion moment — off-shoulder, halter, or keyhole back blouses are very GenZ Navratri. Men in a traditional kediya with mirror-work in matching or complementary colors, paired with churidar or straight-fit ethnic pants. Jewellery is maximalist and non-negotiable — layered necklaces, jhumkas, stacked bangles, kamarbandh, maang tikka, and anklets are all valid simultaneously. The garba floor is also a jewellery showcase. Bandhej and mirror-work are the most catalog-relevant surface treatments. Kids ethnic sets and chaniya cholis are extremely shoppable. Dance-ready hemlines — full floor-length flared skirts twirl best. Day 1 energy is fresh and electric — dress like nine nights of fashion are just beginning.

app.headline: Nine Nights. First Twirl. Go.
lines: Chaniya choli, mirror-work, and garba-ready flares.
Day 1 fit sets the tone for all nine nights.
Stack the bangles, wear the jhumkas, own the circle.

social_media.headline: Garba Season. Twirl Or Go Home.
lines: POV: stepped onto the garba floor in a mirror-work chaniya choli and became the centre of the circle.
Navratri Day 1 fit check — flared lehenga, stacked jewellery, mirror-work everywhere. Nine nights starting now.

events.title: Navratri Day 2
events.description: Navratri Day 2 — the garba circles grow louder, the dandiya sticks fly faster, and the color of the day is green — fresh, auspicious, and alive with the energy of nine nights in full swing.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Navratri Day 2 — color of the day is Green. genZScore 7-9. The garba and dandiya energy is now fully in motion — Day 2 crowd is warmed up and dressed with even more intention than Day 1. Green in the Navratri context spans a beautiful range — emerald, bottle green, lime green, sage, mint, and forest green. The chaniya choli and lehenga choli remain the hero categories. Green with gold zari and mirror-work is an iconic Navratri combination. Bandhej in green tones is deeply traditional and very relevant. The GenZ crowd will push toward deeper emerald and jewel-toned greens over pastel mint. Men in green kediya or kurta with churidar. Jewellery remains maximalist — gold-tone pieces complement green beautifully.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Navratri Day 2 — green is the color and it hits differently on a garba floor. Lead with emerald and bottle green chaniya cholis with gold zari borders and mirror-work detailing — the green-gold combination is one of the most stunning Navratri pairings. Bandhej dupattas and blouses in green tones are deeply traditional and very relevant. Women in a full flared green lehenga with a heavily embroidered or mirror-work blouse — keyhole or off-shoulder blouse designs are the GenZ Navratri signature. Men in a green kediya with churidar or a green kurta with straight ethnic pants — gold buttons and mirror-work details elevate the look. Jewellery remains fully maximalist — gold-tone necklaces, jhumkas, stacked bangles, kamarbandh, and anklets. Green stone or emerald-toned jewellery pieces are uniquely relevant today. The garba floor demands full floor-length flared silhouettes — the twirl factor is everything.

Silver jewellery replaces gold as the dominant metal tone today — a rare shift from the usual gold-heavy Navratri aesthetic. The dominant aesthetic is modest yet luxurious — full coverage but richly embellished. Royal blue chaniya cholis with heavy gold zari borders and mirror-work are the most catalog-relevant pieces — the blue-gold contrast is one of the sharpest and most regal Navratri combinations. Bandhej in indigo and cobalt tones with gold detailing is a deeply traditional craft reference — indigo bandhej chaniya choli is an iconic Day 7 look. Ikat-inspired patterns in blue tones are uniquely relevant for this color day — a nod to India's textile heritage. Women in a full flared sapphire lehenga with a gold embroidered blouse and mirror-work skirt — the keyhole or one-shoulder blouse in gold against blue is a stunning GenZ Navratri combination. Men in a royal blue or cobalt kediya with heavy gold mirror-work and churidar — regal and commanding. Gold jewellery is the dominant metal — blue stone and sapphire-toned pieces are uniquely relevant today alongside classic gold. Three nights remain — dress like the best is still coming.

Ashtami carries a dual energy — deeply devotional AND the most celebratory garba night before the final Navami. Pink on Ashtami is especially meaningful — it represents compassion, femininity, and divine grace. The pink spectrum for Navratri is rich — hot pink, fuchsia, rose pink, blush, magenta, and deep pink. The GenZ crowd goes boldest on Ashtami — this is the night for the most elaborate outfit of the festival. Hot pink and fuchsia are the most garba-floor-dominant shades — vibrant, energetic, and spectacular under lights. Pink with gold zari and heavy mirror-work is one of the most beloved Navratri combinations. Bandhej in pink tones — magenta and hot pink tie-dye — is deeply traditional and catalog-relevant. Contrast blouse in deep green, gold, or red against a pink lehenga creates a stunning Ashtami look. Men in hot pink or magenta kediya with gold detailing — bold and festive. This is the night of maximum jewellery, maximum embellishment, maximum celebration.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Navratri Day 8 — Ashtami is the sacred heart of the festival and pink is its color. This is the night people save their most elaborate outfit for — maximum embellishment, maximum jewellery, maximum intention. Lead with hot pink and fuchsia over softer blush — vibrant, electric shades dominate the garba floor on Ashtami. Hot pink chaniya cholis with heavy gold mirror-work and kundan embroidery are the most catalog-relevant pieces — the pink-gold combination is the most beloved and photographed Navratri pairing. Bandhej in magenta and hot pink tones with gold detailing is a deeply traditional and stunning craft reference. Women in a full flared fuchsia lehenga with a contrast deep green or red embroidered blouse — the color contrast on Ashtami is dramatic and beautiful. Kundan-work and stone-work detailing are uniquely relevant on Ashtami — the most embellished night calls for the most embellished surface treatments. Men in a hot pink or magenta kediya with heavy gold mirror-work and churidar — bold, festive, and Ashtami-appropriate. Jewellery is at its absolute maximum tonight — every piece simultaneously. Layered kundan necklaces, heavy jhumkas, stacked bangles, elaborate kamarbandh, maang tikka with pink stone accents, and anklets. events.description: IPL 2026 kicks off — the world's biggest T20 cricket league returns with packed stadiums, franchise rivalries, celebrity appearances, and the most electric sports entertainment season in India.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: IPL season opener is a massive cultural and fashion moment — genZScore 7-8. IPL is not just cricket — it's entertainment, celebrity culture, and franchise pride rolled into one. The fashion energy is significantly more elevated and diverse than a regular cricket match. Three distinct crowd contexts: (1) stadium attendees — franchise jersey culture, team colors, fan accessories, energetic and loud; (2) watch party crowd — smart casual with team color accents, more relaxed; (3) IPL party circuit — Bengaluru's elite watch parties where people dress up in team colors but make it fashion. IPL franchise colors are more diverse than India blue — every team has its own palette. The GenZ IPL crowd styles their franchise jersey as a fashion piece — layered, cropped, or styled with co-ords. Colors span the full IPL franchise spectrum — Mumbai Indians blue and gold, CSK yellow and blue, RCB red and black, KKR purple and gold, DC blue and red. Caps are essential. Athleisure elevated to fan-fashion is the dominant aesthetic.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: IPL season opener is franchise fashion at full volume — every team has a color code and the crowd lives by it. The franchise jersey is the hero piece but IPL 2026 demands it be styled, not just worn. Women cropping or tying the jersey over a co-ord in matching team colors — a yellow CSK jersey over a blue co-ord, a red RCB jersey over a black set. Men in the franchise jersey over a longline tee with cargo shorts or joggers in matching team colors. Color-blocking in franchise palettes is a strong catalog direction — RCB red and black, MI blue and gold, KKR purple and gold, CSK yellow and blue are all catalog-matchable color combinations. Caps are absolutely non-negotiable — franchise caps or a solid team-color snapback. IPL watch party circuit in Bengaluru elevates the look further — smart casual with team color accents, interesting silhouettes over basic jerseys. Kids franchise jerseys and tees are the most relevant kids category of the season. Socks in team colors. Watches with sporty straps. The opening night energy is maximum — dress like the season is already yours.

events.title: Ram Navami
events.description: Ram Navami marks the birth of Lord Ram — celebrated with temple processions, devotional singing, fasting, and a deeply reverent atmosphere that fills homes and communities with the spirit of dharma and devotion.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Ram Navami is a deeply devotional Hindu festival — genZScore 1-3. It is primarily a religious observance — temple visits, processions, bhajans, and family prayers. The crowd spans all ages with a strong traditional and family focus. The dominant fashion language is pure, auspicious, and devotional — saffron and yellow are the most sacred colors for Ram Navami, representing Lord Ram's divine energy. White and off-white are equally appropriate for the devotional context. Women in simple cotton sarees, salwar suits, or ethnic sets in saffron, yellow, or white. Men in white or saffron kurta-pyjama or dhoti-kurta — clean, pure, and reverential. The procession context means comfortable yet ethnic dressing — nothing too embellished or party-ready. Jewellery is traditional and moderate — gold-tone pieces, simple and meaningful. No western, no fusion, no loud embellishments.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Ram Navami is the saffron and yellow festival — pure, devotional, and deeply auspicious. Saffron is the single most sacred color of the day — a saffron cotton saree or salwar suit with minimal gold detailing is the most appropriate and beautiful Ram Navami look. Yellow is equally auspicious — turmeric yellow ethnic sets and kurtas in cotton or linen feel right for the daytime procession and temple context. Women in crisp cotton sarees in saffron or yellow with gold zari borders, or a clean salwar suit in turmeric tones with chikankari or block-print detailing. Men in white or saffron kurta-pyjama or dhoti-kurta — khadi fabric is uniquely appropriate for Ram Navami's spiritual and cultural resonance. Lightweight breathable fabrics are essential — Ram Navami processions are outdoor and active. Gold-tone jewellery in simple traditional pieces — no heavy embellishments. Simple necklaces, small jhumkas, bangles, and meaningful amulet pendants. Kids ethnic sets in saffron and yellow are highly relevant — Ram Navami is a family festival. The overall energy is pure, reverent, and dressed for devotion.

app.headline: Saffron And Devotion. Ram Navami.
lines: Saffron saree or yellow kurta — both are offerings today.
Cotton, khadi, and quiet reverence — the Ram Navami brief.
Dress for the procession, the prayers, and the people you love.

social_media.headline: Saffron Fits For A Sacred Day.
lines: POV: wore a saffron cotton saree to Ram Navami and the temple glowed warmer.
Ram Navami fit check — saffron or yellow, cotton or khadi, gold jewels, full devotion.

events.description: Sugaray Rayford brings his powerful blues and soul sound live — a night of raw emotion, gritty guitar, gospel-tinged vocals, and the kind of music that makes the whole room feel something deep and real.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Sugaray Rayford is an acclaimed American blues and soul artist — genZScore 4-6. His audience in Bengaluru would be a culturally sophisticated mix — jazz and blues enthusiasts, older millennials, world music fans, and a smaller but very intentional GenZ crowd. Blues and soul concerts have a very specific fashion language — elevated, vintage-inspired, and deeply considered. Think jazz club aesthetic — rich jewel tones, vintage silhouettes, velvet textures, wide-leg trousers, structured blazers, and the kind of dressing that respects the music. This is not a casual event — people dress with intention for a blues legend. Colors are deep and rich — burgundy, deep blue, forest green, black, caramel, rust, and warm browns. The overall aesthetic is sophisticated vintage — referencing the golden era of blues and soul while staying contemporary. Jewellery is statement and warm-toned. Men in structured blazers, tailored trousers, interesting shirts. Women in vintage-inspired dresses, wide-leg suits, or a rich jewel-toned co-ord.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Sugaray Rayford live is the most sophisticated dressing moment of the season — blues and soul music demands you show up with intention. The jazz club aesthetic is the brief — rich jewel tones, vintage-inspired silhouettes, and interesting textures. Women in a deep burgundy or forest green wide-leg trouser suit, a vintage-inspired wrap dress in caramel or rust, or a rich jewel-toned satin co-ord. The wide-leg trouser with a structured blazer is the most on-brand silhouette for a blues concert. Men in a well-fitted blazer over a camp collar or button-down shirt in rich tones — navy, burgundy, or forest green — with tailored trousers. events.title: Holi
events.description: Holi — the festival of colors — is India's most joyful and uninhibited celebration, where streets explode in gulal, water guns drench everyone, music fills every corner, and the rule is simple: wear white, expect everything.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Holi is the most physically intense fashion event of the year — genZScore 7-9. The brief is brutally specific: wear white cotton, get absolutely drenched in color and water, and still look iconic. The GenZ Holi fashion moment has three distinct acts: (1) Pre-Holi — fresh white fits, Instagram-ready before the chaos; (2) During Holi — color-soaked, water-drenched, fully surrendered to the festival; (3) Holi party circuit — evening DJ parties where people show up in fresh festive fits post-cleanup. White is the only base color — everything else is applied by the festival. Fabric is the most critical fashion decision — 100% cotton ONLY, lightweight, quick-dry. Fits must allow full physical movement — running, dancing, water fights. Minimal to zero jewellery — water and color will destroy anything delicate. No synthetic fabrics, no silk, no embellishments, no dark colors. The Holi party evening circuit adds a second fashion moment — bright festive co-ords, ethnic sets, and colorful western pieces for post-Holi celebrations.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Holi has one non-negotiable rule — 100% cotton white only, zero exceptions. The white co-ord is the ultimate Holi party look — a white cotton crop top with white shorts or a white matching set that photographs beautifully before the color hits and looks even better after. Men in a simple white cotton tee with white cotton shorts or a classic white kurta-pyjama — both are iconic. The white kurta is deeply traditional and the most photographed Holi look every year. Fabric is a life decision here — cotton absorbs color into beautiful tie-dye patterns naturally, synthetic fabrics stain permanently and uncomfortably. Fits must allow full movement — no tight silhouettes, no restrictive cuts. Absolutely zero jewellery — color and water will destroy everything. A simple waterproof cap protects hair. Post-Holi evening is a completely separate fashion moment — bright festive co-ords, colorful ethnic sets, and fresh western pieces for the DJ party circuit. Kids cotton whites and ethnic sets are the most relevant catalog category of the entire year for this event.

app.headline: White In. Colors Will Handle The Rest.
lines: Cotton whites that turn into a masterpiece by noon.
White kurta or white crop set — Holi approves both.
Wear something you love getting completely ruined.

social_media.headline: Before Holi Vs. After Holi. Both Iconic.
lines: POV: wore an all-white cotton co-ord to Holi and accidentally became a walking art installation.
Holi fit rule — white, cotton, no jewellery, no regrets. That's literally it.
